problems.title: Mass "phase out" marks an attached Aura/Equipment as *directly* phased out instead of *indirectly*, inverting CR 702.26h
feature: add next-step context and clarification path
problems.issue_body:
## Problem

A multi-step local agent must use what happened in the previous step. It also needs a safe path when the next action is uncertain or multiple targets match. Without that, the loop will either guess or stop with vague errors.

## Proposed behavior

Add a next-step context object that carries recent screen summary, selected tool result, verification result, failure reason, and candidate targets into the next local decision. Add a clarification decision path when the local router cannot safely choose one action.

Clarification cases should include:

- multiple matching targets
- missing required target
- ambiguous user request
- low confidence local decision
- action would require user choice before continuing

## User workflow

When TouchPilot is unsure, it asks a direct question such as “Which Settings item should I open?” instead of executing a risky or random action.

## Local-first impact

This improves small local model reliability by giving it structured context and a non-execution fallback.

## Safety and security considerations

Clarification must not expose raw sensitive screen text. If policy blocks the action, the loop should stop or ask for approval only through the existing approval path, not through generic clarification.

## Acceptance criteria

- Adds a reusable next-step context structure.
- Carries tool result and verification result into the next decision.
- Adds clarification decision type and stop/continue behavior.
- Includes tests for ambiguous target and no-valid-action cases.
- Keeps blocked-policy behavior distinct from clarification behavior.

problems.title: feature: show tool call cards in chat
problems.issue_body:
## Problem

Tool calls are central to TouchPilot, but chat users need a readable view of what tool was selected, why it ran, whether approval was needed, and what happened. Raw logs are too dense for normal use.

## Proposed behavior

Add compact tool call cards inside the chat step timeline. Cards should show tool name, redacted arguments, approval/policy status, result status, short message, and verification status when available.

## User workflow

During a task, the user can quickly scan each action TouchPilot took and see whether it succeeded.

## Local-first impact

Visible local tool calls make the local agent loop auditable without cloud traces.

## Safety and security considerations

All arguments and messages must use the existing redactor. High-risk or blocked policy outcomes should be clearly marked.

## Acceptance criteria

- Tool calls render as compact cards in chat.
- Cards include tool name, redacted args, result, and verification state.
- Approval-required and blocked states are easy to distinguish.
- Long args/messages wrap cleanly on mobile.
- No raw sensitive values are displayed.

problems.title: feature: add bounded local agent loop
problems.issue_body:
## Problem

TouchPilot needs to move from one-shot tool routing to short multi-step local tasks, but the loop must be bounded so it cannot run indefinitely or keep acting after repeated failures.

## Proposed behavior

Add a local observe-decide-act loop with strict run limits. The loop should observe the screen, choose the next local action, execute through the existing policy/tool path, verify the result, and either continue or stop.

Initial stop conditions should include:

- success verified
- max steps reached
- repeated tool failure
- policy blocked
- clarification needed
- no valid next action
- user cancelled

## User workflow

A user asks for a small local task such as opening an app or navigating to a settings subscreen. TouchPilot runs a few safe steps and stops with a clear reason.

## Local-first impact

The loop should use deterministic local routing and the existing local reasoning path. It must not require cloud/API provider configuration.

## Safety and security considerations

Every action must still pass through `DefaultActionPolicy` and existing approval behavior. The loop must stop on blocked or uncertain actions instead of bypassing policy.

## Acceptance criteria

- Adds a bounded local agent loop entry point.
- Supports configurable max steps with safe default.
- Emits structured step records.
- Stops with clear machine-readable and user-facing reasons.
- Includes unit tests for success, max-step, blocked, and failure stop paths.

problems.title: feature: add dismiss_keyboard tool
problems.issue_body:
## Problem

The soft keyboard can cover controls and change the accessibility tree after typing. Agents currently have to guess whether pressing Back is meant to dismiss the keyboard or navigate away, which makes edit flows less reliable.

## Proposed behavior

Add a `dismiss_keyboard` Android tool that hides the soft keyboard when it is visible and reports whether the screen changed.

Expected arguments:

- `timeout_ms`: optional bounded wait for the keyboard to disappear.

## User workflow

After `focus_input` and `type_text`, the agent can call `dismiss_keyboard`, then `wait_for_idle`, then tap the next visible control without relying on a generic Back action.

## Local-first impact

This gives local command routing a precise editing primitive and reduces ambiguous use of `press_back`.

## Safety and security considerations

The tool should only hide input UI. It should not submit text, clear text, or navigate app state beyond keyboard dismissal. If implemented through Back fallback, the result should verify that the keyboard disappeared rather than silently navigating away.

## Acceptance criteria

- Tool is registered in the Android tool catalog.
- Detects keyboard visibility when possible.
- Hides the keyboard without submitting text.
- Returns clear success, already-hidden, and failure outcomes.
- Includes unit tests for result formatting/argument validation and live-test checklist coverage on focused input fields.

problems.title: feature: add agent run detail screen
problems.issue_body:
## Problem

The chat timeline should stay compact, but maintainers and advanced users need a deeper view of a local agent run for debugging and live validation.

## Proposed behavior

Add an agent run detail screen that can be opened from a completed run or logs. It should show the full ordered step list, tool calls, redacted arguments, verification details, stop reason, and trace/export affordance if already supported.

## User workflow

A user or maintainer taps a run summary and inspects the full run details without digging through raw log text.

## Local-first impact

This supports on-device debugging and trust in the local observe-decide-act loop.

## Safety and security considerations

The detail screen must use display-safe/redacted fields and should not expose raw accessibility snapshots unless the user explicitly exports a redacted debug trace.

## Acceptance criteria

- Run detail screen is reachable from chat run summary or logs.
- Shows ordered steps with statuses and timestamps.
- Shows redacted tool args/results and verification details.
- Provides a clear empty/error state if run data is unavailable.
- Does not display raw sensitive screen text.

problems.title: feature: show agent step timeline in chat
problems.issue_body:
## Problem

When a local task runs multiple steps, users need to see what TouchPilot is doing. A hidden loop makes the app feel unpredictable and makes debugging difficult.

## Proposed behavior

Render local agent steps as a compact timeline inside the chat-oriented agent screen. The timeline should show observe, decide, act, verify, clarify, and stop steps as they happen.

## User workflow

The user sends a task, then sees TouchPilot progress through each step without leaving chat.

## Local-first impact

A visible local timeline makes local reasoning inspectable and reinforces that the task is running on-device.

## Safety and security considerations

Timeline text must use redacted display fields. Sensitive args, screen text, or trace data must not be rendered raw.

## Acceptance criteria

- Chat screen renders agent step timeline items.
- Step status is visible: pending, running, success, failed, blocked, clarification needed.
- Timeline updates incrementally during a run.
- Empty and completed states look polished on mobile.
- Uses existing app Material styling conventions.

problems.title: feature: show clarification prompt in chat
problems.issue_body:
## Problem

When the local agent is uncertain, the user should see a clear question and respond in the same chat flow. A plain failure message is not enough for multi-step tasks.

## Proposed behavior

Render clarification requests as assistant chat messages. Where the backend provides options, show them as tappable choices; otherwise allow a normal text reply.

## User workflow

TouchPilot asks “Which item should I tap?” and the user can choose an option or type an answer. The run can then continue with the clarified context.

## Local-first impact

Clarification lets a small local model avoid unsafe guessing while still completing useful tasks.

## Safety and security considerations

Clarification options must be redacted and display-safe. User answers should go through the same policy path before actions resume.

## Acceptance criteria

- Chat renders clarification prompt messages.
- Optional answer choices are shown as tappable controls.
- Text reply path works when choices are absent.
- Clarification response is connected back to the active run state.
- Sensitive option text is redacted.

problems.title: feature: add wait_for_app tool
problems.issue_body:
## Problem

TouchPilot can open an app, but follow-up actions still need a reliable way to wait until the expected app is actually foreground. Without this, agent loops and tool chains can race Android launch transitions and act on the previous screen.

## Proposed behavior

Add a `wait_for_app` Android tool that waits until a target package name or launcher label becomes the foreground app, then returns a structured success/failure result.

Expected arguments:

- `package`: optional Android package name.
- `label`: optional launcher/app label.
- `timeout_ms`: optional bounded timeout with a safe default.

At least one of `package` or `label` should be required.

## User workflow

A local agent can call `open_app({target=Calculator})`, then `wait_for_app({label=Calculator})`, then observe or interact only after the app switch is confirmed.

## Local-first impact

This keeps app-switch synchronization local and deterministic, reducing the need for a larger model to guess whether Android has finished transitioning.

## Safety and security considerations

The tool should be observation/synchronization only. It must not launch apps itself, grant permissions, or bypass policy. Returned app identifiers should go through the normal logging/redaction path where applicable.

## Acceptance criteria

- Tool is registered in the Android tool catalog.
- Supports package-name and label matching.
- Uses bounded timeout and clear failure messages.
- Includes unit tests for argument validation and success/failure result formatting.
- Live-test checklist is updated for open-app plus wait-for-app flow.

problems.title: feature: add open_settings_panel tool
problems.issue_body:
Parent: #75

## Problem

Opening Android Settings by label works, but navigating to specific settings panels by tapping is brittle.

Many common workflows need direct access to Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, Accessibility, app details, notification settings, or system settings screens.

## Proposed behavior

Add an `open_settings_panel` Android tool using Android Settings intents where available.

Supported panels can start small, for example:

- `wifi`
- `bluetooth`
- `accessibility`
- `app_info`
- `notifications`
- `system_settings`

The tool should return unsupported-panel errors instead of guessing.

## User workflow

A user can ask TouchPilot to open a specific Android settings panel directly, reducing brittle tap chains.

## Local-first impact

Direct local intents make common Android workflows more reliable without external services.

## Safety and security considerations

Settings panels can affect sensitive device state. Opening a panel is safer than toggling settings, but it should still be logged and may remain medium risk.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Add `open_settings_panel` to the Android tool catalog.
- [ ] Support an initial allowlist of safe panel names.
- [ ] Use Android Settings intents where available.
- [ ] Return clear failure for unsupported panel names.
- [ ] Do not toggle settings automatically.
- [ ] Add unit tests for panel mapping and live checklist coverage.

## Alternatives considered

Use `open_app` plus `tap` chains. Rejected because common settings navigation should be deterministic and less brittle.

problems.title: feature: add observe_screen_context tool
problems.issue_body:
Parent: #75

## Problem

TouchPilot exposes `observe_screen` as a raw Accessibility tree dump, but Milestone 3 added structured `ScreenContext` for local screen understanding.

Agents and debug tooling need a structured observation tool that returns the normalized context directly.

## Proposed behavior

Add an `observe_screen_context` Android tool that returns a redacted serialized `ScreenContext`.

The output should include:

- app/package/window metadata,
- visible nodes,
- roles,
- bounds,
- click/input/scroll flags,
- sensitive text redacted or marked safely.

## User workflow

The local agent can inspect structured screen context before choosing reliable tool actions.

## Local-first impact

Structured local context is the bridge between Milestone 3 understanding and Milestone 4 reliable control.

## Safety and security considerations

`ScreenContext` can contain sensitive visible text. Output must use display-safe/redacted text by default and avoid leaking raw sensitive values in logs or traces.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Add `observe_screen_context` to the Android tool catalog.
- [ ] Serialize `ScreenContext` in a stable JSON shape.
- [ ] Redact sensitive text by default.
- [ ] Keep `observe_screen` available for raw debug compatibility.
- [ ] Add tests for serialization, redaction, and empty context.
- [ ] Document when agents should prefer structured context over raw dumps.

## Alternatives considered

Only keep `observe_screen`. Rejected because raw text dumps are harder to rank, verify, and evolve safely.

problems.title: bug: `_retrieve` ignores `retrieval.backends` config and hardcodes embeddings-primary
problems.issue_body:
**What happened**

`vouch init` writes `config.yaml` with:

```yaml
retrieval:
  backends: ["fts5", "substring"]
```

but `context._retrieve` (`src/vouch/context.py:32`) always calls
`index_db.search_semantic` (embedding) first, regardless of that config — and
the config list doesn't even include `"embedding"`. Meanwhile ROADMAP 0.1 and
the README still say *"Default stays FTS5; embeddings opt-in via config.yaml."*

So the config knob is decorative, the documented default is wrong, and there's
no supported way to force FTS5/substring.

**Expected**

`_retrieve` honors a config setting (e.g. `retrieval.backend: auto|embedding|fts5|substring`,
default `auto` = embedding→fts5→substring), and ROADMAP/README describe the
actual behavior.

**Acceptance**

- Setting the backend in `config.yaml` changes which retrieval path runs.
- `embedding` is a recognized value.
- Docs match behavior.

problems.title: feat: `vouch approve --batch` for scriptable bulk approval
problems.issue_body:
**What you're trying to do**

Approving a review queue is one `vouch approve <id>` per proposal. For CI /
automation (and clearing a backlog) I want to approve a known set in one call.

This complements the interactive `vouch review` (#83) — that's a human walking
the queue; this is non-interactive and scriptable.

**Acceptance**

- `vouch approve <id1> <id2> …` (or `--batch`) approves multiple proposals.
- One audit event per approved artifact.
- Clear all-or-nothing vs best-effort semantics (documented).

problems.title: Add batch approval for reviewed proposals
problems.issue_body:
## What you're trying to do

When I have already reviewed several pending proposals, I want to approve them in one command, because approving each one separately is repetitive and encourages shell workarounds.

## What you've tried

I can run `vouch approve <id>` repeatedly, or use a pipeline like `vouch pending --json | jq -r '.[].id' | xargs -n1 vouch approve`.

That works, but it is clunky, less discoverable, and easier to misuse than a first-class batch approval command.

## Suggested shape

Add batch approval to the existing approve flow:

`vouch approve --batch <proposal-id>... --reason "reviewed together"`

Each proposal should use the same validation, self-approval checks, artifact creation, and audit behavior as single-proposal approval.

## Compatibility considerations

This should be additive and should not break existing KBs. It should not change the object model, on-disk layout, bundle format, or `kb.*` method surface.

Because it preserves the existing review gate and only extends the CLI, it likely should not require a VEP.

## Alternatives

Keep approving proposals one at a time with `vouch approve <id>`.

Use `vouch review` for an interactive queue.

Document a shell pipeline using `vouch pending --json`, though that is less discoverable and easier to get wrong.

problems.title: feat: vouch expire — garbage-collect stale pending proposals
problems.issue_body:
## What you're trying to do

When multiple agents propose writes over weeks, `.vouch/proposed/` accumulates pending proposals that never get reviewed. Those files are **gitignored**, so they do not show up in PRs and are easy to forget. Reviewers see a growing `vouch pending` list with no clear lifecycle for “we’re never going to look at this.”

I want a first-class way to **expire** old pending proposals: move them to `decided/` with an explicit reason, emit audit events, and honor the config knob that already exists in the init template — without silently deleting history or bypassing the review gate.

**Concrete example:** An agent proposed three claims in March; it is now June and nobody approved or rejected them. Running `vouch expire` should list those three; `vouch expire --apply` should close them as `rejected` with `decision_reason: expired`, so `vouch audit` and `decided/` still explain what happened.

## What you've tried

- **`vouch reject`** — Works per proposal but requires a human reason for each id; does not scale for bulk stale cleanup.
- **Manual deletion of `proposed/*.yaml`** — Loses audit trail and violates the “decided/ is never GCed” model.
- **`review.expire_pending_after_days` in `templates/config.template.yaml`** — Documented (default `90`, `0` = never) but **not read anywhere in code** today.

## Suggested shape

### CLI

```bash
vouch expire              # dry-run: list proposals that would expire
vouch expire --apply      # perform GC
vouch expire --json       # machine-readable output

problems.title: bug: kb.context returns archived/superseded claims; lifecycle mutations never re-index FTS5 status
problems.issue_body:
## What happened

`kb.context` returns claims that have been `archived`, `superseded`,
`contested`, or `redacted` as if they were live — agents receive
retracted knowledge in their context pack. Two compounding bugs
combine to produce this:

**Bug A — `build_context_pack` has no `status` filter.**
`src/vouch/context.py:75-101` walks the hits from `_retrieve` and
appends every match to the context pack. There is no check on
`claim.status`. Once a claim is indexed, it stays in retrieval
forever, regardless of subsequent lifecycle calls.

**Bug B — `store.update_claim` never refreshes the FTS5 row.**
`src/vouch/storage.py:308-313`:

```python
def update_claim(self, claim: Claim) -> Claim:
    if not self._claim_path(claim.id).exists():
        raise ArtifactNotFoundError(f"claim {claim.id}")
    self._claim_path(claim.id).write_text(_yaml_dump(claim.model_dump(mode="json")))
    self._embed_and_store(kind="claim", id=claim.id, text=claim.text)
    return claim
```

The embedding cache is refreshed; the FTS5 row is not. So
`lifecycle.archive`, `lifecycle.supersede`, and
`lifecycle.contradict` — all of which finish with
`store.update_claim(...)` and never themselves call
`index_db.index_claim` — leave `claims_fts.status` stuck at whatever
value it had at first-index time.

`index_db.index_claim` is called by `proposals.approve` (at
`src/vouch/proposals.py:258`) on first approval and by
`health.rebuild_index` during `vouch index` / `vouch doctor`. No
update path keeps it in sync with `lifecycle` mutations.

The compound effect: even a future fix that adds a status filter
to `context.py` would still leak archived claims, because the FTS5
`status` column it would filter on is stale. Both bugs must be
fixed for retrieval to be correct.

This breaks the read-side promise of the KB. The whole point of
`ClaimStatus.{ARCHIVED, SUPERSEDED, REDACTED}` is to remove a
claim from active circulation while keeping its history. Today
those states are decorative.

## What you expected

`kb.context` (and the underlying `build_context_pack`) should
exclude claims whose `status` is `ARCHIVED`, `SUPERSEDED`, or
`REDACTED` from the returned items. `CONTESTED` claims may be
surfaced (per `kb.lint` and `vouch lint`, "contested" is a
caution, not a removal), but the host should be able to see
their status — adding `status` to `ContextItem` lets agents and
hosts decide.

`lifecycle.archive`, `lifecycle.supersede`, and
`lifecycle.contradict` should keep `claims_fts.status` in sync
with the on-disk claim — either by calling `index_claim` directly
or by making `store.update_claim` re-index the FTS5 row in
addition to refreshing the embedding cache.

## Reproduction

```
$ python archived_context_repro.py
work dir: /tmp/vouch-archived-XXXXXXXX

--- after approval (stable claim) ---
items: [('mongodb-is-faster-than-postgres', '«MongoDB» is faster than Postgres')]

--- claim mongodb-is-faster-than-postgres archived ---
on-disk claim.status: archived
FTS5 row after archive: id=mongod…

problems.title: bug: Claim model has no min-evidence validator — uncited claims land via bundle import, put_claim, update_claim
problems.issue_body:
## What happened

The `Claim` model has no min-evidence validator
(`src/vouch/models.py:184-187`), so a claim with `evidence: []` is
schema-valid. As a result, the README and CONTRIBUTING contract —

> Claims must cite sources. A claim without at least one Source /
> Evidence id is a **validation error**, not a warning.

is enforced in exactly one place: `proposals.propose_claim`
(`src/vouch/proposals.py:89-90`). Every other write path to
`claims/<id>.yaml` accepts uncited claims silently:

- **Bundle import** — `bundle._validate_content` defers to
  `Claim.model_validate` (`src/vouch/bundle.py:43,222-228`), which
  accepts `evidence: []`. A hand-crafted bundle with one such claim
  passes `import_check` (`ok=True`, no issues) and `import_apply`
  writes it to disk under the legitimate `bundle_id`. The audit
  log records a clean `bundle.import` event for a KB that now
  contains an uncited claim.

- **`store.put_claim` direct** — `src/vouch/storage.py:283-291`
  iterates `claim.evidence` to verify each cited id exists, but an
  empty list iterates zero times. Lifecycle ops
  (`supersede`, `contradict`, `archive`, `confirm`) and any caller
  that constructs a `Claim` directly can land an uncited one.

- **`store.update_claim`** — `src/vouch/storage.py:308-…` writes
  the YAML without any citation check. A previously-cited claim
  can be mutated to `evidence=[]` and the change persists.

This is exactly the failure mode CONTRIBUTING calls out under
"Things we won't merge":

> Validation relaxations that let claims land without citations.
> A claim without evidence is a `working` note at best — register
> a source.

— except the enforcement was never on the model in the first
place, so every write path *except* the one explicit gate already
qualifies as the relaxed shape the team won't accept.

## What you expected

Constructing a `Claim` with `evidence=[]` raises `pydantic.ValidationError`.
That closes all three bypass paths in one stroke:

- `bundle.import_apply` raises (via `_validate_content`) before
  writing.
- `store.put_claim(Claim(evidence=[]))` raises at the `Claim(...)`
  call.
- `store.update_claim(claim_with_emptied_evidence)` raises the
  moment a caller mutates `claim.evidence = []` and tries to pass
  it back through `Claim(...)` or pydantic re-validation.

The duplicate check inside `propose_claim`
(`if not evidence: raise ProposalError(...)`) can stay as the
nicer user-facing error message, but the load-bearing invariant
moves to the model where it belongs.

## Reproduction

```
$ python uncited_repro.py
work dir: /tmp/vouch-uncited-XXXXXXXX
Bypass 1 (put_claim): SUCCEEDED — uncited claim on disk
  YAML on disk has 'evidence: []' → True
Bypass 2 (update_claim): SUCCEEDED — citations stripped to []

bundle.import_check(uncited): ok=True issues=[]
bundle.import_apply: wrote 1 files
landed claim text: 'shipped via bundle, no citations'
landed claim evidence: []

BYPASS 3 CONFIRMED: bundle import landed an uncited claim — the
README c…

problems.title: bug: import_check accepts bundles whose manifest lists files missing from the tarball (no issue raised)
problems.issue_body:
## What happened

`bundle.import_check` walks two loops in series: one over manifest
entries (to compute `new_files` / `conflicts` / `identical`), and one
over tar members (to schema-validate and now — after #74 / #75 — to
verify the per-member sha256). Neither loop flags the case where
`manifest.json` lists a path that has **no corresponding tar member**.

The symptom is the inverse of `hash mismatch` and is just as silent:

- `import_check` returns `ok=True` for a bundle where, say,
  `manifest.json` claims `claims/c1.yaml` exists with a particular
  sha256, but the tarball contains no `claims/c1.yaml` member.
- `import_apply` then iterates tar members, never reaches the
  manifest-listed-but-missing path, and writes nothing for it. The
  `bundle.import` audit event records a clean success with the
  bundle id, claiming "imported the bundle" — but the resulting KB
  is missing artifacts the manifest promised.

`export_check` already catches this (it walks recorded paths and
checks `tar.getmember(path)`). The same companion check is missing
on the import side — exactly the asymmetry #74 was about, just for a
different attack/corruption shape (omission instead of substitution).

## What you expected

`import_check` should append a `manifest lists missing file: <path>`
issue for any manifest entry without a matching tar member,
mirroring `export_check`. `import_apply` then refuses to import
because `check.issues` is non-empty.

## Reproduction sketch

```python
# Build a bundle whose manifest references claims/c1.yaml but the
# tarball contains only manifest.json.
manifest = {
    "spec": bundle.SPEC_VERSION,
    "bundle_id": "deadbeef",
    "files": [{
        "path": "claims/c1.yaml",
        "size": 16,
        "sha256": hashlib.sha256(b"text: any
").hexdigest(),
    }],
    "counts": {},
    "safety": {"has_proposed": False, "has_state_db": False, "has_audit_log": False},
}
with tarfile.open("missing.tar.gz", "w:gz") as tar:
    mf = json.dumps(manifest).encode()
    info = tarfile.TarInfo(bundle.MANIFEST_NAME)
    info.size = len(mf)
    tar.addfile(info, io.BytesIO(mf))

diff = bundle.import_check(store.kb_dir, "missing.tar.gz")
# Today: diff.ok is True. Expected: diff.ok is False with
# a "manifest lists missing file" issue.
```

## Environment

- vouch version: post-#75 (or any version, since this code path
  is untouched by #72 / #74 / #75)
- Cross-platform; reachable from CLI / MCP / JSONL

## Suggested fix

In `src/vouch/bundle.py` `import_check`, after collecting
`manifest_paths` and walking the manifest entries for the dest
diff, add the missing-member pass that `export_check` already has:

```python
member_names = {
    m.name for m in tar.getmembers()
    if m.isfile() and m.name != MANIFEST_NAME
}
for path in manifest_paths - member_names:
    issues.append(f"manifest lists missing file: {path}")
```

(or equivalently `try: tar.getmember(path) except KeyError: ...`
to mirror `export_check`'s shape exactly).

Add a regression test i…

problems.title: feat: Add `--json` output to `vouch pending`
problems.issue_body:
## What you're trying to do

When I’m reviewing proposals in a multi-agent repo, I want to inspect pending proposals in a machine-readable format so I can group them by agent, count noisy proposal sources, or wire the review queue into shell scripts and CI checks.

The docs already suggest this workflow:

```bash
vouch pending --json | jq -r '.[] | "\(.proposed_by)\t\(.id)"' | sort | uniq -c
```
but vouch pending --json does not appear to exist yet.

## What you've tried
vouch status --json gives a machine-readable summary, but it only includes a pending proposal count, not proposal details.

vouch pending lists pending proposals, but the output is human-readable text, so scripts would need to parse display formatting.

The MCP/JSONL kb.list_pending surface can return structured pending proposal data, but that is heavier than needed for simple CLI automation.

## Suggested shape
Add a --json flag to vouch pending.

Expected behavior:

- vouch pending keeps its current human-readable output.
- vouch pending --json emits a JSON array of pending proposals.
- If there are no pending proposals, output [] and exit 0.
- Proposal entries can use the existing proposal model shape, likely Proposal.model_dump(mode="json").

Suggested test coverage:

- pending proposals render as valid JSON
- empty pending queue renders as []
- existing human-readable output remains unchanged
## Compatibility considerations

This should be additive and should not break existing KBs.

It should not require a VEP if the change stays limited to CLI output. It does not change the object model, kb.* method surface, on-disk layout, bundle format, audit-log shape, or transport behavior.

problems.title: feat: Add deterministic sync/merge for diverged .vouch/ directories
problems.issue_body:
## What you're trying to do

When multiple agents, teammates, branches, or repos each have their own `.vouch/` directory, I want a deterministic way to reconcile them, because today the multi-agent story works best on one shared filesystem.

For example, Team A and Team B may both approve claims in separate repos, then later want to combine their reviewed knowledge. Right now that means bundle export/import or git merge, but conflicts still need manual interpretation and there is no first-class “what will happen if I merge these KBs?” workflow.

## What you've tried

Existing bundle features help move a KB snapshot:

- `vouch export`
- `vouch import-check`
- `vouch import-apply`

These are useful for portability, but they do not fully solve distributed KB convergence. They can identify new, identical, and conflicting files, but they do not provide a semantic merge workflow for diverged `.vouch/` directories, decided proposals, superseded claims, contradictions, or audit implications.

Git merge is also possible, but it leaves vouch-specific conflict handling up to the user.

## Suggested shape

Add a first-class sync/merge workflow, for example:

```bash
vouch sync-check <path-or-bundle>
vouch sync-apply <path-or-bundle> --on-conflict fail|skip|propose

problems.title: Bug: Financial data reset can leave family-scoped records behind
problems.issue_body:
## Problem

The financial data reset path and reset-status endpoint should describe and operate on the same family-scoped data surface. If the reset job only clears a narrow subset of records, family-owned financial, import, reference, provider, or attachment data can remain after reset, and reset status can report completion against a different surface than the destructive job actually deletes.

## Expected behavior

- Reset behavior is centralized in one service with an explicit family-scoped deletion surface.
- Reset status uses the same scope as the reset job.
- Provider cleanup only touches associations that are explicitly safe to reset.
- Reset work is idempotent and does not affect another family.
- API docs describe the reset-status response shape accurately.

## Acceptance criteria

- Replace the inline reset-job deletion list with a reusable family financial reset service.
- Include accounts, entries, transactions, transfers, holdings, balances, rules, budgets, taxonomies, imports, exports, statements, provider items, syncs, and related attachments where applicable.
- Keep destructive queries scoped through the current family or family-owned IDs.
- Update reset-status docs and regression coverage for idempotency and two-family isolation.

Related implementation: #1835

problems.title: Bug: OpenAI Custom URL field clears itself if Model is not filled first
problems.issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
When configuring a custom OpenAI endpoint (e.g., for Gemini or local LLMs) in Settings > General, entering the "Base URL" first causes the input field to aggressively reset/clear itself. The UI throws the error: `OpenAI model is required when custom URI base is configured`. 

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Go to Settings > General > OpenAI.
2. Type or paste a URL into the `Base URL` field.
3. Click away or press tab.
4. The field is immediately cleared and the validation error appears, preventing the user from moving to the `Model` field.

**Expected behavior**
The URL field should retain its inputted string, allowing the user to subsequently fill in the `Model` field. Validation should ideally occur `onSubmit` or without resetting the inputted state `onChange`/`onBlur`.

**Workaround**
The user must manually input the `Model` string *before* pasting the `Base URL` to bypass the reactivity bug.

problems.title: Bug: existing user can lose access to original family/account after upgrade + family sharing flow
problems.issue_body:
## Bug description

A self-hosted user reported that after upgrading from `v0.6.7-alpha` to `v0.7.0-hotfix1`, adding an existing second user account (wife) into a family-sharing setup caused the wife's original account context to disappear and all visible accounts to become the husband's.

The user had **two separate users on the same instance** before the upgrade. After adding the wife's account into the husband's `Family`, the wife's prior data appears to have either:
- been reassigned into the husband's family,
- been orphaned under the previous family, or
- had the original user row destroyed while the historical family/account data may still remain.

There is concern that **~5 months of transactions** may now be inaccessible.

## Likely related history

This appears closely related to the long-standing bug where inviting a user who already belongs to another `Family` can move them into the new family and strand or destroy access to the original one.

Relevant prior work:
- #978 (closed, not merged): "Fix account destruction when user switches families via invitation"
- #1171: Check for pending invitations before creating new Family during SSO log in/sign up
- #1173: Enforce one pending invitation per email across all families
- #1272: Family sharing

During triage, we noticed PR #978 still contained a `@user.destroy` path, which raises the possibility that some flows may still destroy the original user record instead of safely preserving/restoring it.

## Reproduction outline

1. On a self-hosted instance, create user A with Family A.
2. Create user B with Family B.
3. Upgrade from `v0.6.7-alpha` to `v0.7.0-hotfix1`.
4. Add user B into user A's family-sharing setup, expecting B to see both A's and B's accounts.
5. Observe that B instead appears to lose access to B's original family/accounts and now sees only A's accounts.

## Expected behavior

If an existing user who already belongs to another family is added to a sharing/family flow:
- their original family and data should remain accessible,
- the app should not silently reassign or destroy their original account context,
- and any sharing flow should be explicit about whether access is additive vs. rehoming the user.

## Actual behavior

The invited/added user's visible account set becomes the inviter's/husband's family, while the original wife-owned family data becomes inaccessible.

## Recovery angle

In the reported case, recovery may still be possible if the prior `families` row and `accounts.family_id` records still exist and only the `users.family_id` link was changed. We suggested read-only SQL to inspect:
- matching `users` rows by email (including any `+family...` preserved-email variant),
- associated `family_id` values,
- whether the old family still has accounts,
- and related `invitations` rows.

If the old family still exists, recovery may be as simple as reassigning the correct user row back to the original `family_id`, but we should confirm whether the destructive path is …

problems.title: Bug: IdentifyRecurringTransactionsJob debounce gate misses 9 of 14 Syncable provider associations
problems.issue_body:
## Before you start (required)

### General checklist

- [x] I have removed personal / sensitive data from screenshots and logs
- [x] I have searched [existing issues](https://github.com/we-promise/sure/issues?q=is:issue) and [discussions](https://github.com/we-promise/sure/discussions) to ensure this is not a duplicate issue

### How are you using Sure?

- [ ] I was a paying Maybe customer (hosted version)
- [ ] I use it on PikaPod, Umbrel or similar (VPS included)
- [x] I am a self-hosted user (local only)

### Self hoster checklist

- Self hosted app commit SHA (find in user menu): `89f42497`
  - [x] I have confirmed that my app's commit is the latest version of Sure
- Where are you hosting?
  - [ ] Render
  - [x] Docker Compose
  - [ ] Umbrel
  - [ ] PikaPod
  - [ ] Other (please specify)

---

## Bug description

`IdentifyRecurringTransactionsJob#family_has_incomplete_syncs?` (`app/jobs/identify_recurring_transactions_job.rb:45-60`) is the debounce gate that delays `RecurringTransaction::Identifier` until the family's in-flight provider syncs have settled. The gate hand-rolls the list of provider `*_items` associations it polls, and the list is incomplete: it only checks `plaid_items`, `simplefin_items`, `lunchflow_items`, `enable_banking_items`, and `sophtron_items`. Nine other provider integrations that are also `Syncable` and have a `has_many :*_items` declaration on `Family` are silently missing from the gate: `coinbase_items`, `binance_items`, `kraken_items`, `coinstats_items`, `snaptrade_items`, `mercury_items`, `brex_items`, `indexa_capital_items`, `ibkr_items`.

When a sync on one of these nine providers is still in flight, the gate falls through, `RecurringTransaction::Identifier` runs against a partial dataset, and any recurring pattern that needs ≥ 3 occurrences silently misses the in-flight batch. When the late-arriving sync finishes it (correctly) re-schedules the job, but the previous run has already written a `RecurringTransaction` row with a stale `occurrence_count` / `last_occurrence_date`; the second run hits the `find_or_initialize_by` upsert path and the counts written reflect only the in-window entries seen at that moment, not the full set.

The maintainers' own `Sync.for_family` helper (`app/models/sync.rb:61-75`) already enumerates every `*_items` association via `Family.reflect_on_all_associations(:has_many)` filtered by inclusion of the `Syncable` module — that is exactly the helper this gate should delegate to so the list cannot drift again. The same drift pattern has already happened once: the file has had a single edit since creation (Apr 19, 2026, when Sophtron was bolted on as the 5th entry of the now-out-of-date hand-rolled list).

### Bypass / drift surface (current code)

```ruby
# app/jobs/identify_recurring_transactions_job.rb:45-60
def family_has_incomplete_syncs?(family)
  # Check family's own syncs
  return true if family.syncs.incomplete.exists?

  # Check all provider items' syncs
  return true if fami…

problems.title: Bug: Multi-currency cash balances aren't fully imported on SnapTrade
problems.issue_body: ## Before you start (required) ### General checklist - [x] I have removed personal / sensitive data from screenshots and logs - [x] I have searched [existing issues](https://github.com/we-promise/sure/issues?q=is:issue) and [discussions](https://github.com/we-promise/sure/discussions) to ensure this is not a duplicate issue ### How are you using Sure? - [ ] I was a paying Maybe customer (hosted version) - [ ] I use it on PikaPod, Umbrel or similar (VPS included) - [x] I am a self-hosted user (local only) ### Self hoster checklist _Paying, hosted users should delete this entire section._ If you are a self-hosted user, please complete all of the information below. Issues with incomplete information will be marked as `Needs Info` to help our small team prioritize bug fixes. - Self hosted app commit SHA (find in user menu): [enter commit sha here] - [x] I have confirmed that my app's commit is the latest version of Sure - Where are you hosting? - [ ] Render - [x] Docker Compose - [ ] Umbrel - [ ] PikaPod - [ ] Other (please specify) --- ## Bug description When connecting a brokerage account via SnapTrade that holds cash in multiple currencies, only the primary currency cash balance is imported. Additional currency cash positions aren't imported However, the account's "Current account value" will have the correct value. Example: A moomoo Canada account with $500 CAD and $1000 USD in cash only imports the $500 CAD. The USD cash is discarded entirely. ### To Reproduce Be as specific as possible so Sure contributors can quickly reproduce the bug you're experiencing. Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Acquire a brokerage account with both USD and CAD cash balances 2. Link the account through SnapTrade 3. Observe in the Holdings tab only the primary currency's cash balance (CAD in my case) is imported. Other positions (in both currencies) are imported correctly. ### Expected behavior USD and CAD cash balances are imported under holdings ### Screenshots and/or recordings ------ ### From Dosu / Claude Code: Root cause: `SnaptradeAccount#upsert_balances!` selects a single cash entry from the SnapTrade balance response using a priority fallback (account currency → USD → first entry) and stores it in the single `cash_balance` field. All other currency entries are ignored. Proposed fix: Store non-primary-currency cash positions as synthetic cash holdings (leveraging the existing `Security` cash pattern and the holdings table's multi-currency support), while keeping the primary currency cash in `cash_balance` as-is. Affected file: `app/models/snaptrade_account.rb` (lines 129-147) Related: #898 (multi-currency holdings) ------ I was able to "band-aid patch" this by storing the other currency as "synthetic cash holdings" here https://github.com/Badbird5907/sure/pull/1

problems.title: Bug: Global merchant modifications does not apply the user_modified flag
problems.issue_body:
## Before you start (required)

### General checklist

- [x] I have removed personal / sensitive data from screenshots and logs
- [x] I have searched [existing issues](https://github.com/we-promise/sure/issues?q=is:issue) and [discussions](https://github.com/we-promise/sure/discussions) to ensure this is not a duplicate issue
    
### How are you using Sure?

- [ ] I was a paying Maybe customer (hosted version)
- [ ] I use it on PikaPod, Umbrel or similar (VPS included)
- [x] I am a self-hosted user (local only)

### Self hoster checklist

_Paying, hosted users should delete this entire section._

If you are a self-hosted user, please complete all of the information below.  Issues with incomplete information will be marked as `Needs Info` to help our small team prioritize bug fixes.

- Self hosted app commit SHA (find in user menu): ae5e762
  - [x] I have confirmed that my app's commit is the latest version of Sure
- Where are you hosting?
  - [ ] Render
  - [ ] Docker Compose
  - [ ] Umbrel
  - [ ] PikaPod
  - [x] Other (please specify) : Kubernetes

---

## Bug description

When merging merchants, using AI or not, and converting synced merchant to family merchant, the ``user_modified`` flag is not set. The next provider sync will overwrite the merchant, forcing me to remerge again or modify it transaction by transaction.

### To Reproduce

1. Have a synced account from a provider which gives merchant information.
2. Modify merchants in the global merchants page.
3. Resync.

### Expected behavior

All the transactions associated with merchants changes should have the ``user_modified`` flag applied to the merchant category.

Thank you !

problems.title: Bug: Surface actionable Plaid configuration/product-access errors in the UI
problems.issue_body:
## Summary

When Plaid fails during the link flow because the configured account is not enabled for the requested products, Sure currently logs the provider error but does not surface a useful explanation to the user in the UI.

In practice, the user clicks **Link with Plaid**, the modal closes, and nothing actionable appears. The real cause is only visible in logs, for example:

```json
"error_message": "Your account is not enabled for the following products: [\"investments\" \"liabilities\" \"transactions\"]. To request access, visit https://dashboard.plaid.com/overview/request-products or contact Sales or your Account Manager."
```

## Problem

This creates a bad failure mode for self-hosted users:
- the Plaid flow appears broken or silent
- the user cannot distinguish UI bugs from provider configuration problems
- maintainers have to diagnose from logs instead of the app giving an actionable error

## Expected behavior

If Plaid returns a known, user-actionable configuration or product-access error, Sure should surface that message (or a sanitized version of it) directly in the UI near the Plaid linking flow.

## Suggested course of action

### 1. Catch and classify Plaid provider errors
- Identify where Plaid link / token / exchange failures are rescued today
- Preserve structured Plaid error fields when available (`error_type`, `error_code`, `error_message`)
- Introduce a small mapper for known Plaid errors that are safe to show to users

### 2. Surface a user-facing error in the Plaid flow
- Show an inline flash/alert/toast when Plaid setup fails
- Include specific guidance for product-access failures, e.g.:
  - your Plaid account is not enabled for required products
  - request access in the Plaid dashboard
  - verify the app is using the expected Plaid environment/account

### 3. Keep raw errors out of the UI unless safe
- Avoid dumping arbitrary provider payloads directly to end users
- Prefer a sanitized message plus optional technical details in logs

### 4. Improve observability
- Log structured Plaid failure metadata with request/context IDs
- Consider storing the last sync/link error on the provider/item record for admin visibility

## Possible implementation direction

A reasonable first pass would be:
- rescue provider errors at the controller/service boundary for Plaid setup/linking
- map known product-access/configuration errors to a friendly message
- return/render that message in the account linking UI instead of failing silently

## Why this matters

This should reduce support/debug churn substantially because the current behavior makes a configuration problem look like a broken button or modal.

problems.title: Bug: Family#tax_advantaged_account_ids omits HSA depository accounts
problems.issue_body:
## Before you start (required)

### General checklist

- [x] I have removed personal / sensitive data from screenshots and logs
- [x] I have searched [existing issues](https://github.com/we-promise/sure/issues?q=is:issue) and [discussions](https://github.com/we-promise/sure/discussions) to ensure this is not a duplicate issue

### How are you using Sure?

- [ ] I was a paying Maybe customer (hosted version)
- [ ] I use it on PikaPod, Umbrel or similar (VPS included)
- [x] I am a self-hosted user (local only)

### Self hoster checklist

- Self hosted app commit SHA (find in user menu): `0342958a`
  - [x] I have confirmed that my app's commit is the latest version of Sure
- Where are you hosting?
  - [ ] Render
  - [x] Docker Compose
  - [ ] Umbrel
  - [ ] PikaPod
  - [ ] Other (please specify)

---

## Bug description

`Family#tax_advantaged_account_ids` in `app/models/family.rb:243-263` is the ID set that the budget engine uses to **exclude** tax-advantaged account activity from income / expense / cashflow totals. PR #724 (`Exclude tax-advantaged account activity from budget & add provider data quality...`) originated this method and explicitly listed HSA as an in-scope account type ("401k, IRA, HSA, Roth IRA, etc."). The implementation correctly handles `Investment` (via `Investment::SUBTYPES`'s `tax_treatment` metadata) and `Crypto` (via the dedicated `tax_treatment` enum), but silently omits `Depository` — including the `Depository(subtype: "hsa")` variant.

Two facts make this matter in production:

- `Depository::SUBTYPES` at `app/models/depository.rb:9` already includes `"hsa" => { short: "HSA", long: "Health Savings Account" }`, so HSA depositories are constructable from the UI and from data importers today.
- `app/models/plaid_account/type_mappable.rb:35` routes Plaid-reported `depository.hsa` accounts to `Depository` (not `Investment`). A "Fidelity HSA cash" or similar Plaid-linked HSA cash account ends up as a `Depository`, not an `Investment`, which means it never enters the `tax_advantaged_account_ids` filter even though it semantically is one.

`Account#tax_advantaged?` (in the `TaxTreatable` concern) calls `accountable.respond_to?(:tax_treatment)` and reads the value if present. `Investment` and `Crypto` both implement `tax_treatment`; `Depository` does not, so `account.tax_advantaged?` returns `false` for every HSA depository in the system. This propagates symmetrically into `IncomeStatement::Totals`, `FamilyStats`, `CategoryStats`, and `Transaction::Search` — every analytics path that PR #724 was designed to suppress tax-advantaged activity from. HSA contributions and withdrawals show up in monthly expense totals, contaminating the household's actual non-tax-advantaged spending picture.

The fix is symmetric to the existing branches: surface `tax_treatment` on `Depository`, drive it from a class-level subtype list (so future expansion happens in one place), and join `depositories` in `Family#tax_advantaged_account_ids` alongside t…

problems.title: Categories settings index performs repeated category and transaction existence queries
problems.issue_body:
## Summary

The categories settings page currently does repeated database work while rendering the category list. Sentry is grouping this under `CategoriesController#index` as an N+1 query issue: each rendered category can trigger its own subcategory lookup and its own transaction-existence check.

## Observed behavior

When a family has a larger category tree, rendering the index can repeatedly execute queries shaped like:

- look up subcategories for each parent category
- check whether each category has any transactions before choosing the delete flow

That makes the page cost grow with the number of categories instead of doing a small, bounded set of queries for the page.

## Expected behavior

The controller should prepare the data needed by the list once, scoped to the current family, and the view should render from that precomputed data without per-row database checks.

## Notes

This is a performance issue, not a data correctness issue. The fix should preserve the existing category hierarchy rendering and delete-menu behavior while avoiding the per-category query pattern.
